svg.title: SPANN, index time
svg.desc: Database vectors are clustered by k-means into many small cells. Centroids plus a tiny in-memory ANN index over them are stored in RAM. Closure assignment stores each near-boundary vector, shown for x(1), in every sufficiently near cell, so x(1) appears in both cell 1 and cell 2 posting lists on SSD.
svg.text: Index time — offline, once per corpus
closure: a boundary vector is stored in every (1+ε)-near cell — note x(1) appears twice
stored: centroids in RAM for routing, duplicated posting lists on SSD
